relatedness_reasoning: The inbound file contains transaction instructions (debits/credits) from investments to accounts, while the outbound file contains customer product holdings and account balances from accounts to marketing. These represent fundamentally different data domains: one is transactional instruction data, the other is account state/portfolio data. While both mention account_number and currency, the outbound file requires customer_id, product_id, balances, and account metadata that are not present or derivable from transaction instructions alone. The outbound schema describes account holdings state, not transaction processing.
